Tennis backhand — Adrien · Cleo · Julie

AdrienCleoJulie · 5 takes per player  ·  Motive @ 360 Hz  ·  Racket rigid body (centre of string bed) used for impact
At the centre of the string bed, Adrien strikes at 16.1 m/s, Cleo at 14.4 m/s, and Julie at 9.6 m/s. All three play a one-handed backhand. Adrien and Cleo execute the same technique signature — large trunk-pelvis loading, clean proximal-to-distal cascade — and differ mainly in consistency. Julie's signature is qualitatively different: flat chain, near-zero loading, an arm-driven stroke rather than a body-driven one. Cleo is left-handed, the only one in the cohort.

1. Why the racket face, not the hand

2. Posture at impact and racket trajectory

3. Kinematic chain — proximal to distal

4. Trunk–pelvis separation (X-factor)

5. Within-player consistency

6. Summary metrics

PlayerRacket peak — typical (m/s)Racket peak — best (m/s)Hand peak (m/s)Racket / HandX-factor loadingBackswing durationReproducibility (CV)
Adrien16.08 ± 0.9817.755.78 ± 0.402.73×28.7° ± 2.4480 ± 22 ms6.1%
Cleo15.12 ± 1.7416.065.49 ± 0.452.74×25.1° ± 4.0348 ± 29 ms12.1%
Julie9.36 ± 0.7210.883.38 ± 0.282.87×5.6° ± 2.4329 ± 34 ms7.5%
Five takes per player. Racket and hand peaks shown as median ± std (typical performance) — see §11 of the guide; the racket-peak best column is the player's single highest take. Cells are tinted: green = stronger on that metric, red = weaker. Backswing duration is left uncoloured (longer ≠ better; depends on stroke type). Reproducibility CV is the within-player coefficient of variation on the racket peak — lower is more consistent.

7. Key observations

  • Racket peak — typical (m/s): Adrien highest (16.08 ± 0.98) — Julie lowest (9.36 ± 0.72)
  • Racket peak — best (m/s): Adrien highest (17.75) — Julie lowest (10.88)
  • Hand peak (m/s): Adrien highest (5.78 ± 0.40) — Julie lowest (3.38 ± 0.28)
  • Racket / Hand: Julie highest (2.87×) — Adrien lowest (2.73×)
  • X-factor loading: Adrien highest (28.7° ± 2.4) — Julie lowest (5.6° ± 2.4)
  • Reproducibility (CV): Adrien lowest (6.1%) — Cleo highest (12.1%)
Synthesis. Three findings line up across the five figures. (i) On raw output, Adrien hits hardest (16.1 m/s), Julie softest (9.6 m/s), Cleo in the middle (14.4 m/s). (ii) All three players use a one-handed backhand, but on technique they split into two distinct groups, not three. Adrien and Cleo execute the same body-driven signature: clean proximal-to-distal cascade (Fig. 3), large trunk-pelvis loading of +25–30° (Fig. 4), racket peaks 2.7× the hand peak. Julie sits apart with a flat chain, near-zero X-factor, and a shorter racket arc (Fig. 2) — an arm-driven stroke rather than a body-driven one. (iii) The Adrien-vs-Cleo gap is sharper than first read. On swing mechanics the two are essentially the same: Cleo's backswing duration on her four valid takes is 348 ± 33 ms, comparable to Adrien's 480 ± 22 ms — the tempo IS locked. The delta lives in output: her racket peaks span 11.0–16.1 m/s (CV ≈ 12%) where Adrien sits at 6%. Same body motion, variable power transfer to the racket. That's a more specific diagnosis than 'inconsistent overall' — it points at the wrist/contact phase rather than the whole stroke.
Limits of this analysis. Limits of this analysis. n = 1 player per execution level, so the technical signatures describe Adrien, Cleo, and Julie individually, not any broader category. Five takes per player give a fair within-player picture but the between-player gaps could partly reflect skill, conditioning, instructions, or fitness on the day. All three players were confirmed to use a one-handed backhand. Racket impact is detected from peak rigid-body speed (proxy for ball-contact; no ball was tracked) — error ≈ 1–2 frames (3–6 ms). Speed is at the centre of the string bed; a point at the racket tip would be faster by an additional ω×r term.

All 15 takes passed the data-quality probe: worst NaN coverage on a key bone (Hip / Chest / dominant hand / Racket) inside the [-500, +300] ms ROI was 0.00% with longest gap 0 frames — well below the threshold at which marker dropouts could corrupt downstream metrics. Tight ROI-margin takes (impact close to the take start, less than 100 ms slack before the [-500, +300] ms window): cleo_003 (97 ms slack). The ROI was preserved by NaN-padding inside align_on_event; the affected curves are still meaningful but contribute less to the overlay before -500 ms.

Outlier check (±40% from subject median, applied per guide §7 step 5 since n=5): Cleo/cleo (backswing = 800, deviates +136% from the subject's median of 339) — diagnosed as a window-edge artifact (the local-min detector hit the start of the [-800, -100] ms search window because no real local minimum exists inside it; the actual backswing duration for this take is undetermined, not 800 ms). Excluded from backswing aggregate only. Every other take and metric uses all 15 takes; the exclusion is scoped to the affected metric only.
Pipeline: mocap.py + report.py from the project knowledge base. Rigid bodies: Adrien Rigid Body 001, Cleo Racket, Julie Raquette (all centred on the string bed). Vertical axis Y, quaternions (x, y, z, w). Smoothing: Savitzky-Golay window 21, polynomial 3. Analysis window: -500 / +300 ms around impact.
